article_title: EdgeCore Secures $1.5 Billion to Build Two Fully Leased Hyperscale AI Data Centers in Northern Virginia
summary: EdgeCore $1.5B financing for two Sterling, VA AI data centers — Yahoo Finance snapshot is the EU consent wall but the actual press release was verified via WebFetch (every claim confirmed); EIA and Broadband Breakfast snapshots fully verified.

findings.message: Source redirected to different domain: consent.yahoo.com

content_preview.title: EdgeCore Secures $1.5 Billion to Build Two Fully Leased Hyperscale AI Data Centers in Northern Virginia
content_preview.summary: EdgeCore Digital Infrastructure closed $1.5 billion in senior secured term loans for AS01 and AS02 in Sterling, Virginia, two single-tenant facilities totaling 114 MW and 685,000 square feet, with AS01 going live in November 2026 and AS02 in July 2027.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

EdgeCore Digital Infrastructure announced on April 21 that it has closed $1.5 billion in construction financing for two fully leased hyperscale data centers in Sterling, Virginia, according to a press release [carried by Yahoo Finance](https://finance.yahoo.com/sectors/technology/articles/edgecore-digital-infrastructure-secures-1-120000612.html). The two single-tenant facilities, AS01 and AS02, will offer a combined 114 megawatts of critical load across 685,000 square feet, with AS0...

recommendations: Add eia.gov to config/source_allowlist.txt — it's a U.S. government primary source.

Same Yahoo-Finance EU-consent-wall issue as the 2026-05-08 Anthropic/Colossus article — consider a Yahoo-Finance-specific fetch helper that bypasses consent.yahoo.com or routes through Archive.org by default.

editor_notes.content_quality: Mid-length News piece (838 words) with strong structure: Overview / What We Know / A Market Already Under Power Strain / Why a Green Loan Matters / What We Don't Know. Numerical density is high and each statistic carries an inline citation.
editor_notes.source_verification: 3 sources. (1) finance.yahoo.com — snapshot captured the EU consent wall (consent.yahoo.com) rather than the press release. As last-resort verification, the live URL was WebFetched: every claim attributed to Yahoo Finance is confirmed verbatim or as a structural fact — the April 21 announcement, $1.5B total, AS01/AS02 in Sterling, 114 MW critical load, 685,000 sq ft, November 2026 / July 2027 occupancy, Apterra/GIP-BlackRock/ING-as-Green-Loan-agent, Davis Polk & Wardwell / Milbank, Dominion Energy power, Kestler quote verbatim ('Today's financial announcement is especially exciting for EdgeCore as it is directly related to our accelerated growth plans'), 1.4 GW pipeline, Partners Group $185B AUM, and Boyse/Brewer names. (2) Broadband Breakfast (source-1, HTTP 200) — confirms Loudoun County 199 + 117 figures, Dominion 13.3 GW by 2038 vs ~2.8 GW in 2022, $50.1B Dominion capex 2025-2029, 100 MW/80,000 homes equivalence, 4,000+ diesel generators and 11+ GW standby capacity. (3) EIA (source-2, HTTP 200) — confirms verbatim: '23,905 MW in 2025, 23% higher than in 2019', '25,413 MW in the 2025–26 winter season, 45% higher than in the 2019–20 winter season', '5.4% per year over the next 10 years', '30.0 million megawatthours (MWh) between 2019 and 2025, much faster growth than in any other state except Texas', and 'PJM's Dominion zone currently serves the largest concentration of data centers in the world'.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All specifics (financing structure, capacity, sq footage, schedule, lender roster, EIA peak-load numbers, Broadband Breakfast Dominion projections) trace to verified sources. The headline, summary, and Overview lead are all supported.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and analytical. 'Wall of new financing' in the closing paragraph is a fair characterization given the corroborated capital + load figures.
editor_notes.originality: No prior EdgeCore or hyperscale-Virginia coverage in 2026-05. The 2026-04-30 Commonwealth Fusion Virginia article is adjacent but covers fusion permitting, not data center financing.
concerns: Yahoo Finance snapshot is the EU consent wall (consent.yahoo.com), not the press release. Same pattern as the 2026-05-08 Anthropic/Colossus article. Mitigated by live WebFetch verification, but the provenance chain commits the consent-wall snapshot, not the press release. Documented in source_verification_per_url.
recommendations: Consider building a Yahoo-Finance fetch helper that detects the consent redirect and routes through Archive.org to capture the actual article content for provenance.

source_verification_per_url.notes: Snapshot is the consent.yahoo.com cookie wall. Live WebFetch verified the actual press-release content: $1.5B April 21 announcement, AS01/AS02 in Sterling, 114 MW / 685,000 sq ft, Nov 2026 / July 2027 occupancy, Apterra/GIP/ING leads with ING Green Loan agent, Davis Polk / Milbank, Dominion Energy power, Kestler quote verbatim, 1.4 GW pipeline, Partners Group $185B AUM, Boyse & Brewer.

Confirms 199 operating + 117 development data centers in Loudoun County, Dominion 13.3 GW by 2038 vs 2.8 GW in 2022, $50.1B Dominion capex 2025-2029, 100 MW/80,000 homes equivalence, 4,000+ diesel generators / 11+ GW standby capacity.

Confirms verbatim: 30.0 million MWh growth 2019-2025 ('much faster growth than in any other state except Texas'), Dominion zone 23,905 MW summer 2025 / 23% higher than 2019, 25,413 MW winter 2025-26 / 45% higher than 2019-20, 5.4% per-year forecast, 'largest concentration of data centers in the world' phrasing.

editor_notes.verdict_override_rationale: Auto-verdict APPROVE_WITH_CORRECTIONS triggered by (a) allowlist warning on eia.gov (gov primary source — clearly trustworthy), (b) Yahoo Finance consent-wall redirect (snapshot captured the consent page, not the article). Live WebFetch verified every single Yahoo Finance-attributed claim. The article is accurate; the snapshot gap is a fetch pipeline configuration issue, not a content defect. Overriding to APPROVE per the false-positive override path, with clear documentation in source_verification_per_url.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Clean APPROVE on the substance. Strong infrastructure/data-center reporting with verified primary sources (EIA, press release via WebFetch) and well-contextualized power-grid backdrop.
